libraries.description: SMIP JS SDK - generic, tenant-agnostic apiDemoMethods helpers + descriptors (3 mutations + getTypes + getObject).

script_templates.script:
'auto', 'relative' => false, 'detectDebug' => false)); // HTMLHelper::_('script', "$primary_domain/media/com_thinkiq/js/dist/tiq.tiqGraphQL.js", array('version' => 'auto', 'relative' => false, 'detectDebug' => false)); // HTMLHelper::_('script', "$primary_domain/media/com_thinkiq/js/dist/tiq.components.js", array('version' => 'auto', 'relative' => false, 'detectDebug' => false)); // HTMLHelper::_('script', "$primary_domain/media/com_thinkiq/js/dist/tiq.charts.js", array('version' => 'auto', 'relative' => false, 'detectDebug' => false)); require_once 'thinkiq_context.php'; $context = new Context(); $php_api = new TiqUtilities\Model\Script('smip_js_sdk.api_template'); $php_api_file_name = $php_api->script_file_name; // Pulls in the descriptor catalog (apiDemoTools[]) — see smip_js_sdk.api_tools. // SMIP JS SDK contributes generic mutation helpers via apiDemoMethods but // adds NO descriptor entries; this page renders an empty tool list unless // a tenant-specific library has also been included before it (e.g. // taf_carbon_js_sdk.api_tools), in which case those descriptors show up. TiqUtilities\Model\Script::includeScript('smip_js_sdk.api_tools'); ?>

{{pageTitle}} source

SMIP JS SDK contributes generic mutation helpers (apiDemoMethods.updateAttribute, apiDemoMethods.createAttribute, apiDemoMethods.updateObject) but adds no descriptor entries to apiDemoTools. Include a tenant-specific library (e.g. taf_carbon_js_sdk.api_tools) on top of this one to populate this documentation page.

<?php

// Purpose: this script is a generic PHP API dispatch skeleton for any
// SMIP-side script that wants to expose server-side functions to the
// browser (or to other scripts) via tiqJSHelper.invokeScriptAsync().
//
// The pattern is a simple switch on $context->std_inputs->method:
//   - $f          — the requested function name (e.g. "MyTenantMethod")
//   - $a          — the parsed arguments object (from JSON in std_inputs->data)
//   - JsonResponse — Joomla's JSON envelope used to return values to the caller
//
// Tenant-specific libraries that want their own PHP-side methods should
// either fork this template into their own api_template script, or
// `includeScript` it and add `case "<MethodName>":` blocks to the switch
// below. The skeleton here ships with no cases — SMIP JS SDK is JS-only
// (mutations + getTypes); PHP cases are the tenant's to fill in.

require_once 'thinkiq_context.php';
$context = new Context();

TiqUtilities\Model\Script::includeScript('smip_js_sdk.guzzle_client');

use Joomla\CMS\Response\JsonResponse; // Used for returning data to the client.
if (!defined('JPATH_BASE')) define('JPATH_BASE', dirname(__DIR__));

$f = isset($context->std_inputs->method) ? $context->std_inputs->method : '';
$a = isset($context->std_inputs->data)   ? json_decode($context->std_inputs->data) : '';

use TiqUtilities\GraphQL\GraphQL;

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
// Tenant-specific PHP-side functions go here. Define them above and
// add a matching `case "<MethodName>":` block to the switch below.
//
// Example (commented out — copy & adjust):
//
// function MyTenantMethod($someArg){
//     $client = new Guzzler();
//     return $client->GetAsync("some/path/$someArg");
// }
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------

switch ($f){
    // case "MyTenantMethod":
    //     $returnObject = MyTenantMethod($a->someArg);
    //     die(new JsonResponse($returnObject));
    //     break;
}

<script>
//
// SMIP JS SDK — generic, tenant-agnostic apiDemoMethods helpers + a
// minimal apiDemoTools catalog of generic queries and mutations.
//
// What this library carries:
//   • apiDemoMethods._buildPatchLiteral(patch)            — patch builder
//   • apiDemoMethods.updateAttribute({ attributeId, patch }) — generic mutation
//   • apiDemoMethods.createAttribute({ ownerId, attributeTypeDisplayName }) — generic mutation
//   • apiDemoMethods.updateObject({ id, patch })         — generic mutation
//   • apiDemoMethods.getTypes(args)                       — generic tiqTypes query
//   • apiDemoMethods.getObject(args)                      — generic single-object fetch
//
// Plus matching apiDemoTools descriptors so the doc page renders an
// interactive form for each one. The mutation forms are dangerous —
// a wrong attribute id and a `null` patch silently clears that
// column; treat the doc page as a developer-only debug surface.
//
// Tenant-specific libraries (e.g. `taf_carbon_js_sdk`) include this
// SDK first, then add their own descriptors and handler methods on
// top of the same `apiDemoTools` / `apiDemoMethods` globals. The
// merge is additive — both libraries use the `(typeof X !== 'undefined')
// ? X : default` guard so load order doesn't matter:
//
//     var apiDemoTools   = (typeof apiDemoTools   !== 'undefined') ? apiDemoTools   : [];
//     var apiDemoMethods = (typeof apiDemoMethods !== 'undefined') ? apiDemoMethods : {};
//     Object.assign(apiDemoMethods, { /* this library's methods */ });
//
// To consume both layers from a display script:
//
//     <?php
//     TiqUtilities\Model\Script::includeScript('smip_js_sdk.api_tools');
//     TiqUtilities\Model\Script::includeScript('taf_carbon_js_sdk.api_tools');
//     ?>
//

// Additive globals — load order doesn't matter; later loads merge in.
var apiDemoTools   = (typeof apiDemoTools   !== 'undefined') ? apiDemoTools   : [];
var apiDemoMethods = (typeof apiDemoMethods !== 'undefined') ? apiDemoMethods : {};


// =====================================================================
// Descriptor catalog — what shows up in the doc page
// =====================================================================

apiDemoTools.push(

    //
    // Mutation — UpdateAttribute. ⚠ DESTRUCTIVE: writes the supplied
    // patch fields onto an existing attribute. Pass attributeId and a
    // patch object (e.g. {"floatValue": 12.5} or {"datetimeValue": null}
    // to clear a column). Multiple patch keys are allowed.
    //
    {
        key:         "updateAttributeViaJs",
        name:        "UpdateAttributeViaJs",
        category:    "Mutation (JS)",
        description: "Update an attribute's value(s) by id. DESTRUCTIVE — writes to the SoR. attributeId is a digits-only attribute node id. patch is a JS object with one or more AttributePatch fields: floatValue, datetimeValue, stringValue, enumerationValue, objectValue. Pass `null` for a field to clear that column to GraphQL null; omit a field (or use `undefined` in code) to skip it. The handler is apiDemoMethods.updateAttribute, which lives here in this library.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "updateAttribute",
        inputs: [
            { key: "attributeId", argName: "attributeId", label: "Attribute id (digits): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "patch", argName: "patch", label: "Patch (JSON object): ", type: "text",
              default: '{"floatValue": 0}',
              parse: "json", showJsonPreview: true }
        ],
        output: { kind: "single" }
    },

    //
    // Mutation — CreateAttribute. Creates a fresh attribute on an
    // existing owner object by attribute-type displayName. Used when
    // an attribute slot hasn't been auto-instantiated yet on a fresh
    // object.
    //
    {
        key:         "createAttributeViaJs",
        name:        "CreateAttributeViaJs",
        category:    "Mutation (JS)",
        description: "Create a new attribute on an owner object by attribute-type displayName. Returns the freshly-created { id, displayName }. Useful when an attribute slot hasn't been auto-instantiated on a freshly-created instance — call this first, then call UpdateAttribute on the returned id. ownerId is a digits-only object node id; attributeTypeDisplayName is the exact displayName of the attribute type to instantiate (matched against the type's typeToAttributeTypes).",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "createAttribute",
        inputs: [
            { key: "ownerId", argName: "ownerId", label: "Owner id (digits): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "attributeTypeDisplayName", argName: "attributeTypeDisplayName", label: "Attribute type displayName: ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" }
        ],
        output: { kind: "single" }
    },

    //
    // Mutation — UpdateObject. ⚠ DESTRUCTIVE: writes the supplied
    // patch fields onto an existing object. Common patches:
    //   { "importance": -260420 }   — sort key (lower = first)
    //   { "displayName": "..." }    — rename
    //   { "description": "..." }    — set/clear description
    //
    {
        key:         "updateObjectViaJs",
        name:        "UpdateObjectViaJs",
        category:    "Mutation (JS)",
        description: "Update an object's properties by id. DESTRUCTIVE — writes to the SoR. id is a digits-only object node id. patch is a JS object with one or more ObjectPatch fields: importance (int), displayName (string), description (string). Pass `null` for a field to clear it; omit a field to skip it. Common at TAF Carbon for setting `importance: -<yymmdd>` so newest runs sort to the top of instance lists.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "updateObject",
        inputs: [
            { key: "id", argName: "id", label: "Object id (digits): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "patch", argName: "patch", label: "Patch (JSON object): ", type: "text",
              default: '{"importance": 0}',
              parse: "json", showJsonPreview: true }
        ],
        output: { kind: "single" }
    },

    //
    // Query — GetTypes. List tiqTypes with optional filters and
    // optional related-data subselections. Each include-* toggle adds
    // a fragment to the GraphQL query (more data = more cost), so the
    // default-OFF posture keeps the round-trip lean for "just show me
    // the types" use cases. Two search strings (typeNameSearch +
    // instanceNameSearch) push case-insensitive substring filters
    // server-side via PostGraphile's includesInsensitive.
    //
    // Use this to:
    //   * Enumerate a tenant's type system (typeNameSearch='').
    //   * Find a type's attribute schema before spawning instances
    //     (typeNameSearch='Lignin Lot', includeTypeAttributes=true).
    //   * List instances of a type ('LA Storage', includeInstances=true).
    //   * Scope to a sample by name ('LA Storage', includeInstances=true,
    //     instanceNameSearch='LA-260424', includeInstanceAttributes=true).
    //   * Walk supertype/subtype/composition relationships.
    //
    {
        key:         "getTypesViaJs",
        name:        "GetTypesViaJs",
        category:    "Query (JS)",
        description: "List tiqTypes with optional filters and optional related-data subselections. typeNameSearch filters types by case-insensitive displayName substring; empty input returns every type. The 6 toggles add subselections — each is more data over the wire, defaults all OFF for a lean response. Use this to enumerate a tenant's type system, find a type's attribute schema before spawning instances (set includeTypeAttributes), list instances of a type (set includeInstances + optional instanceNameSearch + optional includeInstanceAttributes), or walk supertype / subtype / composition. Returns the raw tiqTypes list with whatever subselections are turned on. The shape is intentionally not flattened — this is an exploration tool, not a row-builder.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "getTypes",
        inputs: [
            { key: "typeNameSearch", argName: "typeNameSearch", label: "Type name search (substring, optional): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "includeTypeAttributes", argName: "includeTypeAttributes", label: "Include type attributes (typeToAttributeTypes)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeInstances", argName: "includeInstances", label: "Include instances (objectsByTypeId)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "instanceNameSearch", argName: "instanceNameSearch", label: "Instance name search (substring, optional, only when instances on): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "includeInstanceAttributes", argName: "includeInstanceAttributes", label: "Include attributes on each instance? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeSubTypes", argName: "includeSubTypes", label: "Include subtypes? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeSuperType", argName: "includeSuperType", label: "Include supertype (subTypeOf)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeComposition", argName: "includeComposition", label: "Include composition (childEquipment)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false }
        ],
        output: { kind: "array" }
    },

    //
    // Query — GetObject. Fetch a single object by id with optional
    // related-data subselections. The everyday workhorse for type-bound
    // display scripts that need to load their own instance — pass
    // context.std_inputs.node_id and pick which slices of the graph
    // come along: attributes (default ON, all five value-variant
    // columns + relativeName), parent (partOf), children
    // (childObjects), and type (a small {id displayName relativeName}
    // pointer). Mirrors the Python pattern of "get this object plus
    // a couple of related rows in one round-trip".
    //
    {
        key:         "getObjectViaJs",
        name:        "GetObjectViaJs",
        category:    "Query (JS)",
        description: "Fetch a single object by id with optional related-data subselections. nodeId is a digits-only SMIP object node id (REQUIRED). The 4 toggles control what comes back alongside the object's identity fields. includeAttributes (default ON) returns all five attribute value-variants — floatValue / datetimeValue / stringValue / objectValue / enumerationValue — plus enumerationName and relativeName, so any caller can pick the variant that matches the attribute's data_type without a second query. includePartOf adds the parent object (id / displayName / typeId / typeName). includeChildObjects adds direct children. includeType adds a small type pointer. Returns the object dict with whatever subselections are turned on, or null if no object with that id was found. Use this in any type-bound display script that needs to load its own node.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "getObject",
        inputs: [
            { key: "nodeId", argName: "nodeId", label: "Node id (digits): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "includeAttributes", argName: "includeAttributes", label: "Include attributes? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: true },
            { key: "includePartOf", argName: "includePartOf", label: "Include partOf (parent object)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeChildObjects", argName: "includeChildObjects", label: "Include childObjects? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeType", argName: "includeType", label: "Include type pointer? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false }
        ],
        output: { kind: "single" }
    }

);


// =====================================================================
// apiDemoMethods — the four mutation helpers (always direct-callable,
// also handlers for the three Mutation descriptors above) plus the
// getTypes and getObject query handlers.
// =====================================================================

Object.assign(apiDemoMethods, {

    // Build a GraphQL `patch: { ... }` literal from a JS object.
    //
    // Distinguishes three states per field:
    //   • undefined  — skip entirely (field doesn't appear in patch).
    //   • null       — emit `field: null` (clears the column to GraphQL null).
    //   • everything else — JSON-encode and emit as the value.
    //
    // Numbers are emitted bare (so float values arrive as floats, not
    // string literals); strings get JSON.stringify quoting; booleans
    // emit `true` / `false`. This is the same semantics the Python
    // `update_attribute` uses with its `_UNSET` sentinel for skip vs.
    // explicit-null. Returns the rendered `{ ... }` literal as a string,
    // ready to interpolate into a mutation body.
    _buildPatchLiteral: function(patch){
        const parts = [];
        for (const key of Object.keys(patch || {})) {
            const v = patch[key];
            if (v === undefined) continue;             // skip
            if (v === null)        { parts.push(`${key}: null`); continue; }
            if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') {
                parts.push(`${key}: ${v}`);
            } else {
                // String, or anything else — round-trip through JSON.stringify
                // so embedded quotes / backslashes are properly escaped.
                parts.push(`${key}: ${JSON.stringify(String(v))}`);
            }
        }
        return `{ ${parts.join(', ')} }`;
    },

    // Update an attribute's value(s) by id.
    //
    // Args shape:
    //   { attributeId, patch }
    //     attributeId — digits-only attribute node id (REQUIRED).
    //     patch       — plain JS object of fields to set:
    //                     { floatValue: 12.5 }
    //                     { datetimeValue: "2026-04-20T10:00:00Z" }
    //                     { datetimeValue: null }      ← clears to GraphQL null
    //                     { stringValue: "abc" }
    //                     { enumerationValue: "1" }
    //                     { objectValue: '{"x":1}' }   ← stringified JSON for object-type attributes
    //                     { objectValue: null }        ← clears object-type attribute
    //                   Multiple patch keys are allowed; each maps to
    //                   one field on the AttributePatch input. Fields
    //                   set to `undefined` are skipped, `null` clears
    //                   the column. At least one key must be present.
    //
    // Returns the GraphQL response (full envelope), so callers can
    // inspect `data.updateAttribute.clientMutationId` if they want.
    updateAttribute: async function(args){
        const attributeId = String((args && args.attributeId) || '').trim();
        const patch       = (args && args.patch) || {};
        if (!attributeId || !/^\d+$/.test(attributeId)) {
            throw new Error('updateAttribute: attributeId must be digits-only; got ' + JSON.stringify(attributeId));
        }
        if (Object.keys(patch).length === 0) {
            throw new Error('updateAttribute: patch must contain at least one field');
        }
        const patchLit = apiDemoMethods._buildPatchLiteral(patch);
        const mutation = `
            mutation UpdateAttribute {
              updateAttribute(input: {
                id: "${attributeId}",
                patch: ${patchLit}
              }) { clientMutationId }
            }
        `;
        return await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(mutation);
    },

    // Create a new attribute on an owner object by attribute-type
    // displayName. Used by display scripts when an attribute slot
    // hasn't been auto-instantiated yet (e.g., a fresh object that
    // doesn't have a particular attribute on it yet).
    //
    // Args shape:
    //   { ownerId, attributeTypeDisplayName }
    //     ownerId                  — digits-only object node id (REQUIRED).
    //     attributeTypeDisplayName — exact displayName of the attribute
    //                                type to instantiate (REQUIRED).
    //
    // Returns the freshly-created `{ id, displayName }` attribute dict.
    createAttribute: async function(args){
        const ownerId = String((args && args.ownerId) || '').trim();
        const name    = String((args && args.attributeTypeDisplayName) || '').trim();
        if (!ownerId || !/^\d+$/.test(ownerId)) {
            throw new Error('createAttribute: ownerId must be digits-only; got ' + JSON.stringify(ownerId));
        }
        if (!name) {
            throw new Error('createAttribute: attributeTypeDisplayName is required');
        }
        const mutation = `
            mutation CreateAttribute {
              createAttribute(input: {
                attribute: {
                  ownerId: "${ownerId}",
                  attributeTypeDisplayName: ${JSON.stringify(name)}
                }
              }) {
                attribute { id displayName }
              }
            }
        `;
        const resp = await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(mutation);
        return resp && resp.data && resp.data.createAttribute && resp.data.createAttribute.attribute || null;
    },

    // Update an object's properties (importance, displayName, description, …).
    //
    // Args shape:
    //   { id, patch }
    //     id    — digits-only object node id (REQUIRED).
    //     patch — plain JS object of fields to set on the ObjectPatch input:
    //               { importance: -260420 }
    //               { displayName: "RL-260420-1" }
    //               { description: "...", importance: 5 }
    //             Same null/undefined semantics as updateAttribute's patch.
    //
    // Returns the GraphQL response envelope.
    updateObject: async function(args){
        const id    = String((args && args.id) || '').trim();
        const patch = (args && args.patch) || {};
        if (!id || !/^\d+$/.test(id)) {
            throw new Error('updateObject: id must be digits-only; got ' + JSON.stringify(id));
        }
        if (Object.keys(patch).length === 0) {
            throw new Error('updateObject: patch must contain at least one field');
        }
        const patchLit = apiDemoMethods._buildPatchLiteral(patch);
        const mutation = `
            mutation UpdateObject {
              updateObject(input: {
                id: "${id}",
                patch: ${patchLit}
              }) {
                object { id importance displayName description }
              }
            }
        `;
        return await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(mutation);
    },

    // Query — list tiqTypes with optional filters and optional
    // related-data subselections. Mirrors the Python automation
    // helpers `get_type_by_display_name` (single-type lookup) plus
    // some of the patterns we'd otherwise write by hand inline:
    // listing types, finding a type's attribute schema, listing
    // instances under a type, walking sub/super-type relationships.
    //
    // Args shape (all optional except as noted):
    //   {
    //     typeNameSearch:            String — case-insensitive substring on tiqType.displayName
    //                                         (PostGraphile `includesInsensitive`). Empty = all types.
    //     includeTypeAttributes:     Boolean — add `typeToAttributeTypes { id displayName dataType importance }`.
    //     includeInstances:          Boolean — add `objectsByTypeId(filter: ...) { id displayName description }`.
    //     instanceNameSearch:        String — case-insensitive substring on instance.displayName,
    //                                         only used when includeInstances is true.
    //     includeInstanceAttributes: Boolean — add `attributes { ... }` to each instance,
    //                                         only used when includeInstances is true.
    //     includeSubTypes:           Boolean — add `subTypes { id displayName relativeName }`.
    //     includeSuperType:          Boolean — add `subTypeOf { id displayName relativeName }`.
    //     includeComposition:        Boolean — add `childEquipment { id displayName }`.
    //                                         Best-effort field shape; refine if the schema differs.
    //   }
    //
    // Returns the raw `tiqTypes` array from the GraphQL response.
    // Each entry is `{ id, displayName, relativeName, description,
    // fqn, idPath }` plus whatever optional subselections were turned
    // on. Intentionally not flattened — this is an exploration tool.
    getTypes: async function(args){
        args = args || {};
        const typeNameSearch            = String(args.typeNameSearch     || '').trim();
        const includeTypeAttributes     = !!args.includeTypeAttributes;
        const includeInstances          = !!args.includeInstances;
        const instanceNameSearch        = String(args.instanceNameSearch || '').trim();
        const includeInstanceAttributes = !!args.includeInstanceAttributes;
        const includeSubTypes           = !!args.includeSubTypes;
        const includeSuperType          = !!args.includeSuperType;
        const includeComposition        = !!args.includeComposition;

        // Optional case-insensitive filter on the outer tiqTypes — pushed
        // down server-side. Omitted entirely when empty so all types come
        // back without a redundant predicate.
        const typeFilter = typeNameSearch
            ? `(filter: { displayName: { includesInsensitive: ${JSON.stringify(typeNameSearch)} } })`
            : '';

        // Inner instance filter — only used when includeInstances is on.
        const instFilter = (includeInstances && instanceNameSearch)
            ? `(filter: { displayName: { includesInsensitive: ${JSON.stringify(instanceNameSearch)} } })`
            : '';

        // Each subselection is its own conditional fragment, spliced into
        // the query body if its toggle is on. Empty string when off — so
        // the rendered query stays as small as the caller asked for.
        const typeAttrSel = includeTypeAttributes
            ? `typeToAttributeTypes {
                    id displayName dataType importance
                  }`
            : '';

        const instAttrSel = includeInstanceAttributes
            ? `attributes {
                        id displayName floatValue datetimeValue stringValue enumerationName
                      }`
            : '';

        const instSel = includeInstances
            ? `objectsByTypeId${instFilter} {
                    id displayName description
                    ${instAttrSel}
                  }`
            : '';

        const subTypesSel = includeSubTypes
            ? `subTypes { id displayName relativeName }`
            : '';

        const superTypeSel = includeSuperType
            ? `subTypeOf { id displayName relativeName }`
            : '';

        // childEquipment is the SMIP "this type is composed of these
        // equipment subtypes" relation. Field shape on this build is
        // best-effort — if the schema exposes more fields (multiplicity,
        // equipmentType, etc.) extend the selection here.
        const compositionSel = includeComposition
            ? `childEquipment { id displayName }`
            : '';

        const query = `
            query GetTypes {
              tiqTypes${typeFilter} {
                id displayName relativeName description fqn idPath
                ${typeAttrSel}
                ${instSel}
                ${subTypesSel}
                ${superTypeSel}
                ${compositionSel}
              }
            }
        `;

        const resp = await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(query);
        return (resp && resp.data && resp.data.tiqTypes) || [];
    },

    // Query — fetch a single object by id with optional related-data
    // subselections. The everyday workhorse for type-bound display
    // scripts that need to load their own instance.
    //
    // Args shape:
    //   {
    //     nodeId:              String  — digits-only SMIP object node id (REQUIRED).
    //     includeAttributes:   Boolean — default TRUE. Returns every value variant
    //                                    on each attribute (floatValue, datetimeValue,
    //                                    stringValue, objectValue, enumerationValue)
    //                                    plus enumerationName and relativeName, so
    //                                    callers can pick the column matching the
    //                                    attribute's data_type without a second query.
    //     includePartOf:       Boolean — default FALSE. Adds the parent object (id /
    //                                    displayName / typeId / typeName).
    //     includeChildObjects: Boolean — default FALSE. Adds direct children
    //                                    (id / displayName / description /
    //                                    typeId / typeName).
    //     includeType:         Boolean — default FALSE. Adds a small type pointer
    //                                    (id / displayName / relativeName).
    //   }
    //
    // Returns the object dict with whatever subselections are turned
    // on, or null if no object with that id was found. The shape is
    // intentionally not flattened — same shape as `Query.object` in
    // GraphQL with the optional fields tacked on.
    //
    // Use this in any type-bound display script that needs to load
    // its own node from `context.std_inputs.node_id`. For loading a
    // PSD Result with the PSD-specific flat-field shaping, use
    // taf_carbon_js_sdk's `getPsdResults` instead.
    getObject: async function(args){
        args = args || {};
        const nodeId              = String(args.nodeId || '').trim();
        const includeAttributes   = (args.includeAttributes !== false);  // default TRUE
        const includePartOf       = !!args.includePartOf;
        const includeChildObjects = !!args.includeChildObjects;
        const includeType         = !!args.includeType;

        if (!nodeId || !/^\d+$/.test(nodeId)) {
            throw new Error('getObject: nodeId must be digits-only; got ' + JSON.stringify(nodeId));
        }

        // Each subselection is its own conditional fragment, spliced in
        // only when the toggle is on — keeps the round-trip lean.
        const attrsSel = includeAttributes
            ? `attributes {
                    id displayName relativeName
                    floatValue datetimeValue stringValue objectValue
                    enumerationValue enumerationName
                  }`
            : '';

        const partOfSel = includePartOf
            ? `partOf { id displayName typeId typeName }`
            : '';

        const childrenSel = includeChildObjects
            ? `childObjects { id displayName description typeId typeName }`
            : '';

        const typeSel = includeType
            ? `type { id displayName relativeName }`
            : '';

        const query = `
            query GetObject {
              object(id: "${nodeId}") {
                id displayName description typeId typeName partOfId importance
                ${typeSel}
                ${attrsSel}
                ${partOfSel}
                ${childrenSel}
              }
            }
        `;

        const resp = await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(query);
        return (resp && resp.data && resp.data.object) || null;
    },

});

</script>

<?php

use GuzzleHttp\Client;
use GuzzleHttp\Exception\GuzzleException;

class Guzzler
{
    // Generic Guzzle wrapper. The endpoint and api-key strings here are
    // sample values — re-target by editing the two private fields below
    // for the API you're integrating with. The header / URL-param key
    // patterns are kept as commented-out reminders; uncomment whichever
    // one matches your target API's auth model.
    private $apiKey       = "abc-***-***-123";
    private $api_endpoint = "https://example.com/api/v1/";
    private $headers      = array(
        "Content-Type" => "application/json",
        // use this in case the api key goes into the header
        // "apiKey" => $this->apiKey
    );

    private $client;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $this->client = new Client();
    }

    public function GetAsync($query, $getRawContent = false)
    {
        try{
            // use this in case the api key goes into the url parameters
            // if(str_contains($query, '?')){
            //     $query = $query . '&apiKey=' . $this->apiKey;
            // } else {
            //     $query = $query . '?apiKey=' . $this->apiKey;
            // }

            $response = $this->client->request('GET', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            if($getRawContent){
                // use this to return text or xml
                return $content;
            } else {
                // use this for normal json
                $result = json_decode($content);
                return $result;
            }
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }

    public function PutAsync($query, $body = null)
    {
        try{
            $response = $this->client->request('PUT', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers, 'body' => json_encode($body)]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            $result = json_decode($content);
            return $result;
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }

    public function DeleteAsync($query)
    {
        try{
            $response = $this->client->request('DELETE', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            $result = json_decode($content);
            return $result;
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }

    public function PostAsync($query, $body = null)
    {
        try{
            $response = $this->client->request('POST', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers, 'body' => json_encode($body)]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            $result = json_decode($content);
            return $result;
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }

    public function PatchAsync($query, $body = null)
    {
        try{
            $response = $this->client->request('PATCH', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers, 'body' => json_encode($body)]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            $result = json_decode($content);
            return $result;
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }
}
